audit_items.proof_trace: The proof technically fails: `list` (strdup'd at L4629) is not freed on the early returns at L4647 (invalid IPv6) and L4665 (invalid port). However, both call sites (L5746-5748, L5761-5763) immediately call `exit(EX_OSERR)` when `server_sockets` returns non-zero, so the process terminates and the OS reclaims all memory. `settings.inter` is set from the `-l` CLI argument at startup only — it is not network-attacker-controlled at runtime.
